channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Fed Chief Kevin Warsh declines to hint at July rate decision, but says inflation 'too high'
item.description: The Fed chairman joined a panel of other central bank leaders Wednesday at the ECB Forum on Central Banking in Sintra, Portugal.

item.title: U.S. won’t renew USMCA, opening door for negotiations with Canada and Mexico
item.description: President Donald Trump's "primary" issue with USMCA centers on America's trade deficits with Canada and Mexico, a senior administration official said.

item.title: Trump Accounts for kids launch July 4: What parents need to know
item.description: Here's a breakdown of how Trump Accounts work, who qualifies for free money and when the funds can be withdrawn.

item.title: Trump says outside funds 'run my money' after disclosure shows billions in 2025 revenue
item.description: The 927-page annual financial disclosure form shows Trump's purchases and sales of hundreds of companies' stocks, including Nvidia, Amazon and Microsoft.

item.title: Automakers report mixed U.S. sales results as hybrid vehicles drive market
item.description: Second-quarter U.S. new vehicle sales show a sharp divide, with automakers offering hybrid models largely outperforming those without them.

item.title: Trump administration's limits on student loan forgiveness program are blocked. What to know
item.description: Trump's policy that narrowed eligibility for a popular student loan forgiveness program will not go into effect, after two federal judges blocked the rule.

item.title: UBS sees opportunities in these defensive stocks. They also pay dividends
item.description: UBS thinks these dividend-paying defensive names are poised to move higher.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: Meta, General Mills, Datadog, Sandisk & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves midday.

item.title: Bitcoin's summer swoon creates unique trade in Strategy
item.description: Outright shorting bitcoin or high-beta crypto equities after a steep decline carries immense tail risk.

item.title: Trump wants delay but E. Jean Carroll wants to gets paid — now: Court filing
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday denied a request by President Donald Trump that it review a jury's verdict that he sexually abused and defamed the writer.

item.title: Lamborghini reveals new Urus performance hybrid SUV after ditching EVs
item.description: Lamborghini is calling the new Urus SE Performante the "fastest Super SUV in the world."

item.title: Employers who laid off workers citing AI are already starting to regret it
item.description: Companies are realizing artificial intelligence can't do everything after all, prompting them to rehire employees to grow their businesses

item.title: Prolific tech analyst Dan Ives is exiting Wedbush for a new venture
item.description: Ives said the new firm will seek to build what he described as a "modern merchant bank."

item.title: Palantir's Karp bashes OpenAI, Anthropic token model: 'Something has gone completely wrong'
item.description: Karp says skyrocketing token costs are forcing companies to choose open-weight models and prioritize efficiency over so-called tokenmaxxing

item.title: Healthy Returns: Walmart, CVS step in to help seniors navigate Medicare coverage of obesity drugs
item.description: Medicare has started covering obesity drugs for the first time – and companies like Walmart and CVS Health are playing an important role for patients.

item.title: EBay doesn't need Ryan Cohen for its comeback. How to profit from the turnaround
item.description: Tony Zhang breaks down how he's using options to capitalize on a comeback in eBay.

item.title: Meta pops 10% as company makes cloud push to sell excess AI compute power capacity
item.description: The new business is a welcome signal for some investors who have been uneasy about the company's infrastructure spending plans.

item.title: Inflation peaked in May as energy prices fell in June, Kalshi traders think
item.description: Speculators on the prediction market platform give less than 30% odds to inflation peaking above 4.2% in 2026.

item.title: Retrofitted Qatari jet takes flight as Air Force One for Trump's trip to North Dakota
item.description: The refurbished Boeing 747 with luxury features, worth $400 million, was gifted by Qatar, something that raised ethical concerns.

item.title: 2026 laggards shine in Wednesday's mixed stock market that kicked off the third quarter
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Dan Ives goes bullish on SpaceX, touting reusable, super heavy Starship rocket as linchpin for success
item.description: The rocket and satellite communications manufacturer first soared, and then fell back toward its $135 debut price in June.

item.title: How to choose graduate student loans after the federal student aid overhaul
item.description: Federal borrowing options tightened on July 1. Here's how to compare graduate student loans and choose wisely.

item.title: U.S. auto industry faces increased uncertainty without extension of USMCA trade deal
item.description: A major issue for automakers is the deal's rules of origin, which determine where a product comes from and which goods are eligible for preferential treatment.

item.title: Kroger to buy grocer Giant Eagle in $1.65 billion deal as competition heats up
item.description: Kroger said on Wednesday it would buy supermarket chain Giant Eagle ⁠in a $1.65 billion deal, as it looks to scale amid intense ​competition.

item.title: PlayStation will end physical disc production for new games in 2028
item.description: PlayStation will end physical disc production for new games in 2028, the company announced Wednesday morning.

item.title: The $20,000 new vehicle is all but extinct—what the most affordable new car looks like now
item.description: Why the $20,000 new car disappeared, and what budget-conscious buyers can expect today.

item.title: Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June, less than expected, ADP reports
item.description: Companies added slightly fewer workers than forecast, with hiring targeted heavily toward healthcare-related sectors.

item.title: Tech leads first half stock gains — but the biggest winners weren't in the U.S.
item.description: U.S. Big Tech stocks enjoyed strong gains — despite a sharp sell-off at the end of June — but were largely outperformed by their international counterparts.

item.title: South Korean government discriminated against Coupang, U.S. companies, House report finds
item.description: The House Judiciary Committee said the South Korean government discriminated against Coupang and other U.S. companies, in a new report.

item.title: Bulls are betting big on this global ETF that's deep in a bear market
item.description: U.S. stocks just keep winning, with the Nasdaq closing out its best quarter since 2020. Across the globe in China, things couldn't be more different.
